qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Cross-host Git Credential Leak via Exact-Path Repository Match
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets Git credential scoping in `src/poetry/utils/authenticator.py` and `src/poetry/vcs/git/backend.py`, not the advisory's wheel entry path containment bug in `src/poetry/installation/wheel_installer.py`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Project-local `.pth` Auto-Exec via `.poetry/plugins` Startup Loading
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets project-local plugin bootstrap through `site.addsitedir()` in `src/poetry/plugins/plugin_manager.py`, not the advisory's wheel installer write-to-disk path traversal.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's wheel-installation root cause: untrusted wheel entry paths reaching `WheelDestination.write_to_fs()` in `src/poetry/installation/wheel_installer.py` without a containment check before disk write.
evidence: The advisory and patch point to `src/poetry/installation/wheel_installer.py`, where Poetry 2.3.2 built `target_path = Path(self.scheme_dict[scheme]) / path` and 2.3.3 added `resolve()` plus `is_relative_to()` containment checks before writing.
The qualifying finding `cross_host_git_credential_leak` instead describes credential reuse across Git hosts in `src/poetry/utils/authenticator.py` and `src/poetry/vcs/git/backend.py`.
The qualifying finding `project_plugin_pth_autoexec` instead describes startup execution of checked-in `.pth` files via `PluginManager.add_project_plugin_path()` and `site.addsitedir()`.

The session's only archive write finding is the non-qualifying tar-sdist extraction issue in `src/poetry/utils/helpers.py`; the session checklist explicitly cleared the ZIP family and did not report the advisory's wheel-entry write path.
